business.headline: Bajaj Finance raises FD rates up to 7.40%: Should you lock in now?The company has increased rates by up to 45 basis points, with the biggest jump seen in longer tenures. This means investors looking to park money for a few years could now earn better returns.

Markets end lower as crude stays high, rupee hits record lowMarkets closed lower on Thursday as high crude prices and a weak rupee weighed on sentiment. The slide highlighted persistent inflation and capital flow concerns, even as IT and pharma offered limited support.

Bajaj Finserv announces dividend, reports strong FY26 growthBajaj Finserv reported record FY26 income and profit and announced a special dividend with its Q4 results. The update also marked the completion of Allianz's stake exit, leaving its insurance businesses fully Bajaj-owned.

India-NZ FTA opens doors, but only prepared firms will benefitSiddharth Shankar says the India-New Zealand FTA offers big openings for trade and talent. He argues only firms with strong systems, compliance and execution will truly benefit.

8th Pay Commission: What worker bodies have demanded ahead of salary reviewWorker representatives laid out pay, pension and service demands in the first 8th Pay Commission meeting in Delhi. The staff side said the talks ended positively but pressed for wider consultation and more meetings.

OnEMI Technology IPO: Weak Day 1 response, is it risky for long-term investors?Kissht IPO was subscribed just 0.25 times on Day 1, indicating a slow start. The retail portion saw particularly weak interest, with subscriptions at only 0.06 times.

Where should a 25-year-old invest to build wealth: SIPs, stocks or crypto?At 25, with time on your side, what's the smartest way to invest, i.e., SIPs (Systematic Investment Plans), stocks or crypto? Let's have a look.
Big EPFO update: Minimum pension may rise 7.5 times, ATM withdrawals soonThe government is mulling a sharp increase in the minimum pension under the Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS-95), say sources.

From TDS mismatches to capital gains errors: 3 risks under Income Tax Act 2025The Income Tax Act, 2025 is now in force, and for many companies, the transition seems smooth so far. But could small gaps like TDS mismatches and capital gains errors turn into bigger compliance issues?

Rupee sinks to record low as oil spike and Fed stance pile on pressureThe rupee slipped to a record low against the US dollar as crude oil prices surged and the Federal Reserve stayed hawkish. The move underlines pressure from India's rising import bill, firmer dollar demand and tighter global financial conditions.

Sensex down 400 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The BSE Sensex was down 1,215.91 points, or 1.54%, at 76,280.45 around 10:58 am, while the Nifty 50 fell 366.70 points, or 1.51%, to 23,811.25.
Sensex opens 700 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IndiGo down 2%The &P BSE Sensex was down 947.08 points to 76,549.28, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 317.60 points to 23,860.05 as of 10:01 am.

JPMorgan asked Vis Raghavan to go, Citi snapped him up for $52 million in 2 daysVis Raghavan moved from JPMorgan Chase to Citigroup's top investment banking role over a single weekend in February 2024. The rapid switch, later detailed by the Financial Times, sharpened attention on his exit and his rising clout at Citi.

Infosys won't cut jobs despite AI shift, says CEO Salil ParekhInfosys CEO Salil Parekh said the company does not plan layoffs despite the AI shift. The stance sets it apart from peers and underscores a hiring-and-reskilling push.

Crude oil tops $120, highest since 2022: What it means for markets and rupeeDisruptions around the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil transit route, have added to the uncertainty, taking crude oil prices to their highest levels since mid-2022.

8th Pay Commission update: Has the last date been extended amid employee demands?The Commission is holding a three-day consultation session from April 28 to April 30. During these meetings, representatives from various employee groups are discussing key issues related to pay, allowances and service conditions.
Oil hits $115: Prices surge to highest since 2022 amid Iran tensionsOngoing tensions around Iran and fears of supply disruptions are pushing crude prices to levels not seen in nearly two years.

Rajiv Bajaj to step down from Bajaj Finance board at end of termRajiv Bajaj's tenure as a non-executive director will conclude at the close of the company's upcoming annual general meeting, in line with regulatory requirements.

Odisha bank case explained: Rules for withdrawing money after deathOnce an account holder dies, the account is no longer treated as a normal account. Any access to funds becomes a claim settlement process, not a simple withdrawal.
Sensex, Nifty end higher as Q4 earnings boost outweighs crude oil surgeSensex ends 609 points higher, Nifty tops 24,100; RIL up 3%

entertainment.headline: BTS ARIRANG comeback fuels HYBE's record-breaking quarter. Details HYBE posted its highest-ever first-quarter revenue after BTS returned with ARIRANG. The surge spread across touring, merchandise and Weverse, showing the strength of its fan ecosystem.

Bombay High Court dismisses PIL against Raja Shivaji title, no stay on film release The Bombay High Court refused to stay the release of Raja Shivaji over objections to its title. The bench said the late PIL was motivated and found nothing derogatory in the film's name.

Karan Johar to debut at Met Gala 2026? Here's what we know Karan Johar is reportedly set to attend the Met Gala 2026, with official confirmation still awaited. His expected debut comes as Indian names continue to gain visibility at the global fashion event.

The Devil Wears Prada 2 review: Perfectly polished. That's allRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 The Devil Wears Prada 2 review: Nearly two decades later, Miranda Priestly returns as Runway fights for relevance in a digital-first media world. The sequel uses that struggle to examine how fashion, journalism and influence now bend before metrics.

Priyanka, Katrina or Nayanthara: Are Tumbbad 2 makers eyeing an A-list name? Tumbbad 2 is exploring a leading female star for a pivotal role as work on the sequel moves ahead. The talks add fresh intrigue to the film, which is set for December 2027 theatrical release.

Office Romance trailer: Jennifer Lopez leads a risky love story with Brett Goldstein The trailer for Office Romance introduces Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein as colleagues drawn to each other at work. It sets up a choice between following their feelings and protecting the company Jackie has built.

Pavitra Punia shuts down trolls over Mouni Roy surgery claims Pavitra Punia has defended Mouni Roy and lashed out at trolls over surgery speculation. In a video on social media, she said personal choices should be respected and accused suggestive captions of fuelling needless chatter.

Kareena reacts after Karisma's children get relief in Sunjay Kapur's asset case Kareena Kapoor extended support to her sister Karisma by sharing a hopeful message after the Delhi High Court ordered status quo on Sunjay Kapur's estate. The ruling preserves the assets during the will dispute and places Priya Kapur's claims under closer scrutiny.

Pagglait 2 confirmed: Sanya Malhotra returns, shoot to begin in August Pagglait 2 has been greenlit, with Sanya Malhotra and director Umesh Bist returning. The sequel adds another follow-up title to Netflix India's slate.

Kartavya: SRK shares release date of his cop drama starring Saif Ali Khan. See date Saif Ali Khan's cop drama Kartavya is finally set to premiere on Netflix. Shah Rukh Khan shared the release date of the drama backed by his production house, Red Chillies Entertainment.

KD The Devil X review: Mass love for Dhruva Sarja, fans call film absolute rampage KD: The Devil has opened to strong fan chatter on X and mixed critical reviews. The split response turns attention to its stronger second half, theatrical highs and uneven screenplay.

At 76, Nana Patekar's tricep dips leave the internet impressed A video of actor Nana Patekar doing tricep dips has gone viral on social media. Celebrities, including Shilpa Shetty and Badshah, have reacted to the clip on Instagram.

Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaram to skip May 15 release? Here's what we know Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaram may be postponed from its May 15 release amid IPL scheduling concerns. The reported June 4 plan has stirred fan curiosity, though the makers have not confirmed any change.

Sonali Kulkarni shares 5-hour Mumbai traffic nightmare: This is scary Sonali Kulkarni said she was stranded for more than five hours in a traffic jam on Mumbai's Mankhurd bridge while travelling to Pune. Sharing a video from the spot, she called the situation scary and asked the police for an update.

Kara review: Dhanush's heist film knows the drill, but misses the jackpotRelease Date:Thu Apr 30, 2026 Kara movie review: Director Vignesh Raja's Kara, starring Dhanush, Mamitha Baiju and Suraj Venjaramoodu, is a film about a robber caught in a life-altering situation that pulls him back into crime. It has some solid sequences, but doesn't quite come together as a whole.

Hanuman Chalisa to a black thread: Priyanka Chopra's bag is full of surprises Actor Priyanka Chopra opened her tote bag in a new video, revealing everything inside. From a black thread to an EpiPen, she explained why each item matters

Yami Gautam says Aditya is unaffected by Dhurandhar success Yami Gautam, in an interview, said that Dhurandhar's success has not changed Aditya Dhar or their lives. She said the film's run shows audiences are backing strong scripts and returning to cinemas.

Raja Shivaji eyes biggest Marathi opening, advance booking crosses Rs 2.5 crore Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji is poised to post the biggest opening ever for a Marathi film on May 1. Strong bookings from Maharashtra could push the historical drama past existing records.

Release Dhruva Natchathiram on or before June 15: Court clears Vikram's film The Madras High Court has laid down a framework to enable Dhruva Natchathiram's release by June 15. The order puts financial oversight in place while pending dues and exhibitor talks continue.

general news.headline: Mamata Banerjee warns of 'life-and-death' fight if EVMs are tampered withMamata Banerjee visited the EVM strongroom at Sakhawat Memorial School after reports of alleged irregularities. Her visit followed Trinamool Congress protests over ballot handling and warnings against any tampering.

Live: Will fight all my life, says Mamata Banerjee after 3-hour strongroom vigilWest Bengal Polls Live Updates: A controversy has erupted over strongrooms after TMC alleged ballot tampering, citing a viral video, while the Election Commission denied wrongdoing, saying all EVMs and ballots are securely sealed and monitored.

Tampering charge, chaos, Mamata Banerjee guards strongroom: TMC vs BJP in KolkataKolkata witnessed high drama as TMC alleged strongroom irregularities, prompting Mamata Banerjee to spend over three hours inside a Bhabanipur facility, vowing to fight all my life over EVM concerns.

Little brown boy: JPMorgan exec accused of drugging, sexually abusing stafferA former JPMorgan employee, who is an Asian, has sued executive director Lorna Hajdini, alleging sexual abuse, racial harassment and intimidation. The bank has denied the claims, saying no evidence against Hajdini was found after an internal probe.

BRICS at risk of imploding, reveals Delhi meetThere is a wide rift in the BRICS, of which India is a founding member, due to the war in the Middle East. There was no joint statement after the recent meeting in New Delhi that was described as "very tense". India as the BRICS president will have to do a tight rope walk ahead of the crucial summit in September.

Trump to finally get Nobel Peace Prize? US President may be among 287 nomineesDonald Trump is expected to be among the nominees for the Nobel Peace Prize. Leaders of Cambodia, Israel, and Pakistan have stated that they nominated him, citing his efforts to resolve conflicts.

Hyderabad techie dies by suicide, blames wife's 'multiple affairs' in noteA 36-year-old software engineer reportedly drowned after jumping into a lake after accusing his wife of multiple affairs. He left a 19-page note, claiming his wife had affairs with three men.

After TACO, Trump's flip-flops on Hormuz have earned him a new name: NACHOWall Street has coined a new label for Donald Trump's shifting stance on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and dealing with Iran. NACHO, seemingly, justifies his back-and-forth approach.

The sequel uses that struggle to examine how fashion, journalism and influence now bend before metrics.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

BJP to wrest Bengal from TMC with 192+ seats, predicts Today's ChanakyaThe BJP will wrest control of West Bengal from the ruling TMC after 15 years in a major political shift, predicts Today's Chanakya. According to the survey, the BJP is likely to get 192+ seats, while the TMC may secure 100+ seats.

Sorry, mumma has to die: UP woman ends life amid digital arrest ordealIn her suicide note, the 28-year-old victim wrote that she was blackmailed by one person, after which the family members checked her phone and found several calls that kept her under digital arrest.

Rain, thunder and dust storms: Which states get hit hardest on May Day?May Day will bring heavy rain and lightning to east India, a hot dry spell to Delhi and continued pre-monsoon showers across the south. Here is a state-by-state breakdown of what to expect.

Electoral fraud: TMC alleges BJP opened ballot boxes in strongroom, posts videoThe party further alleged that attempts to influence the election had escalated from name deletion and voter intimidation to alleged tampering with electronic voting machines (EVMs), calling it an act of desperation.

Deceived, used: US journalist as Pak event turns into govt-bashing showAn informal media gathering in Pakistan involving foreign reporters, who were stationed for the US-Iran talks, turned into a full-blown controversy. What was supposed to be a meet-and-greet event turned into a political spectacle after two Pakistani journalists used the platform to criticise the government and the military.
Explained: Controversy over Pete Hegseth's wife's Rs 4k White House dressA dress worn by Pete Hegseth's wife, Jennifer Rauchet, at the White House Dinner has sparked an online debate.

Vijay's TVK the disruptor-in-chief? Today's Chanakya exit poll predicts 63 seatsThe Today's Chanakya exit poll projects a strong debut for TVK, estimating 63 ± 11 seats and a 30 per cent (±3 per cent) vote share.

6 drown after cruise boat with 30 onboard sinks in Madhya Pradesh dam; rescue onEmergency response teams rushed to the site and managed to rescue 15 people from the water, while four bodies have been recovered so far.

The secret weapon that Iran says can give US a 'heart attack'Iran has hinted at unveiling a new weapon, which it said might give its enemies a 'heart attack', as talks with the US have hit a dead end. Experts have hinted that it might be the Hoot super torpedo that can slice through warships in no time.

India built its own CRISPR cure for sickle cell. It could cost just Rs 50 lakhIndia has developed BIRSA 101, its first indigenous CRISPR gene therapy for sickle cell disease, using a more precise molecular tool than the globally approved Casgevy and at a projected cost up to 20 times lower.

1 litre of ethanol needs 10,000 litres water: Hidden cost of India's fuel pushIndia's total ethanol production capacity stands at 1,822 crore litres, with a disproportionate share concentrated in already water-stressed states like Delhi and Maharashtra.
Bengaluru rain: What fuelled the intense hailstorm? Will it happen again?Such pre-monsoon showers are normal for April but serve as a reminder of how quickly weather can change in the city.

EVMs in all strongrooms secured, sealed: EC rebuts Trinamool's tampering claimThe Election Commission dismissed allegations made by the TMC over a video purportedly showing ballot boxes being opened before the May 4 counting, asserting that all strongrooms remained sealed and under secure protection.

sports.headline: Gujarat Titans take sweet revenge as Bengaluru endure rare off day IPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Gujarat Titans beat the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by four wickets at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Chasing 156, Shubman Gill's men romped home in 15.5 overs.

Virat Kohli gives Shubman Gill death stare, fiery send-off in Ahmedabad: Watch GT vs RCB, IPL 2026: Virat Kohli brought the intensity with an animated send-off to Shubman Gill, but Gujarat Titans stayed on top after Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder dented RCB in Ahmedabad.

Virat Kohli hits out at umpires after Jason Holder's catch to dismiss Rajat Patidar GT vs RCB, IPL 2026: Virat Kohli was not happy after Rajat Patidar's controversial dismissal against Gujarat Titans, with replays raising questions over Jason Holder's catch and the third umpire's review.

Virat Kohli dominates Kagiso Rabada with boundary blitz, GT pacer answers with revenge IPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Virat Kohli dominated Kagiso Rabada early with a stunning burst of five consecutive boundaries in IPL 2026, but the GT pacer fought back strongly. Rabada had the last laugh by dismissing Kohli for 28.

Was Riyan Parag let off after vaping? Fans call out hypocrisy after escaping ban Riyan Parag's dressing-room vaping incident has sparked backlash after the IPL fined him 20 per cent of his match fee, with fans questioning whether the Rajasthan Royals captain was punished lightly.

MCC defends umpires in Angkrish Raghuvanshi Obstructing the Field dismissal call The MCC defended the umpire's decision to give Angkrish Raghuvanshi out obstructing the field against Lucknow Super Giants, stating it aligns with long-standing laws that penalise wilful changes of running direction.

Indonesia's Thomas Cup exit, a historic first in 68 years Indonesia's shock Thomas Cup group-stage exit ends a 68-year run of dominance, exposing a rare crack in badminton's most enduring empire and possibly marking a defining shift in the sport's power balance.

Lungi Ngidi returns to bowling for Delhi capitals after recovering from head injury IPL 2026: Lungi Ngidi has returned to Delhi Capitals training after the head injury he suffered against Punjab Kings. The footage offers relief, but concussion protocol could still keep him out of the Rajasthan Royals match.

GT vs RCB Highlights: Gujarat beat Bengaluru by 4 wickets in Ahmedabad Gujarat (GT) vs Bengaluru (RCB) Highlights, IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans earned two crucial points after beating the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by four wickets on Thursday at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Jensi Kanabar, 14, rewrites history books, surpasses Sania Mirza's all-time record Jensi Kanabar became the youngest Indian female to reach a professional singles quarter-final at 14 years and five months, surpassing Sania Mirza's long-standing record with a composed run at the ITF W15 New Delhi.

Cristiano Ronaldo five UCL gesture goes viral after Al Ahli fans' title jibe Cristiano Ronaldo answered Al Ahli fans by holding up five fingers after Al Nassr's 2-0 win. The exchange went viral and sharpened the bad blood around a match already clouded by refereeing complaints and on-pitch tension.

Release Hardik Pandya: Mumbai Indians told to reset after 3 mellow years in IPLSimon Doull has urged Mumbai Indians to consider releasing Hardik Pandya if they don't think he will be able to lead the team into the next cycle. Mumbai Indians have had a tough time in IPL 2026. They are rooted to the 9th spot with just two wins in eight games.

Fresh trouble for Riyan Parag? RR captain escapes ban, but women's group seeks probeA women's group has sought an inquiry into visuals purportedly showing Riyan Parag with a vape-like device during an IPL match. It says the incident could normalise a banned product among young viewers and wants accountability.

Riyan Parag escapes ban for vaping during IPL match, fined by BCCIIPL 2026: The Board of Control for Cricket in India has fined Riyan Parag 25% of his match fee for vaping during an IPL match in Mullanpur, with the Rajasthan Royals captain escaping a stricter punishment.

Watch: Venkatesh Prasad's child-like joy in Bengaluru hailstorm, tastes it tooFormer India cricketer Venkatesh Prasad stopped his car during Bengaluru's sudden hailstorm and shared a playful video online. The clip struck a chord as the rare spell cooled the city and briefly turned parts of it white.

IPL business first, cricket next? Sri Lanka great's massive claim on run-festsIPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad's spin bowling coach Muttiah Muralitharan said flat wickets and the IPL's design are fuelling relentless 200-plus totals. He argued the league prioritises entertainment, sponsors and viewer interest over cricket development after SRH's victory over MI with eight balls to spare.

Travishek Blueprint: The simple secret behind IPL's most destructive opening pairIPL 2026: Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma powered Sunrisers Hyderabad's chase of 245 against Mumbai Indians with another explosive opening stand. Their contrasting strengths and uncomplicated method continue to drive SRH's winning run.

Call Klaasen and add him to 2027 World Cup squad, Pietersen tells South AfricaIPL 2026: Kevin Pietersen has urged Cricket South Africa to convince Heinrich Klaasen to reverse his retirement, citing his match-winning form for Sunrisers Hyderabad. Klaasen, has been in a sensational form in the IPL scoring 414 runs at a strike-rate of 15741, placing him second in the Orange Cap race behind teammate Abhishek Sharma.

Gujarat Titans hope to erase mistakes of the past vs red-hot RCBGT vs RCB, IPL 2026 preview: Gujarat Titans are set to face Royal Challengers Bengaluru in their reverse fixture on Thursday. GT need to come out of their shell and adapt the modern T20 template if they want to beat a red-hot RCB.

Champions League: Arteta cries foul over VAR call as Arsenal held by AtleticoChampions League semi-final first leg: Coach Mikel Arteta hit out after Arsenal's penalty was overturned by VAR in Madrid. He said the decision denied a potentially decisive win.

Bumrah entitled to have a bad IPL season: Kieron Pollard's big message to criticsIPL 2026: Kieron Pollard defended Jasprit Bumrah after the Mumbai Indians pacer returned 0 for 54 against Sunrisers Hyderabad at Wankhede Stadium on Wednesday. His remarks highlighted the scrutiny on cricketers as Bumrah's lean IPL 2026 run continued.

Video: Vinod Kambli's Wankhede appearance during IPL match warms heartsIPL 2026: Vinod Kambli made a rare appearance at Wankhede Stadium during Mumbai Indians' match against Sunrisers Hyderabad on Wednesday, acknowledging fans who wished him well. The outing eased fresh health concerns and highlighted the support around the former India batter.

IPL Play of the Day: SRH prove T20 is a bowlers' game too with Wankhede masterclassMI vs SRH, IPL 2026: Sunrisers turned the game at the death, restricting Mumbai Indians to 243 when the home side looked set to cross 260. Ehsan Malinga shone in the final overs, while Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma later laid the foundation for a record chase at Wankhede Stadium on Wednesday.

After teammates, Hardik Pandya snaps at MI fans for supporting opponentsIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya has now turned his focus from teammates to the stands, calling out Mumbai Indians fans for backing opposition teams during this season. The MI captain admitted it "hurts" hearing rival chants at Wankhede after another disappointing home defeat.

No respite for MI as 243 not enough against rampant SRH at WankhedeIPL 2026: SunRisers Hyderabad extended their winning run in the Indian Premier League by recording the 4th highest chase in the history of the tournament. Chasing 244, Heinrich Klaasen anchored the SRH innings and took the team over the line in just 18.4 overs on Wednesday.

Is this the end for Suryakumar Yadav? Fans fume over MI star's IPL 2026 slumpIPL 2026: Suryakumar Yadav fell for 5 off 5 balls against Sunrisers Hyderabad at Wankhede. His lean run this season has raised concerns over Mumbai Indians' middle order and triggered mixed fan reaction.

IRGC-linked Iran football body chief lands in Canada, sent back hours laterMehdi Taj, the IRGC-linked president of Iran's football federation, was deported from Canada just hours after arriving for this week's FIFA Congress, reigniting debate over Ottawa's tough stance on figures linked to the Iranian regime.

Ayush Shetty, HS Prannoy grab gritty wins as India finish 2nd in group-stageIndia vs China badminton, Thomas Cup: Ayush Shetty and HS Prannoy grabbed gritty wins in their singles matches as India lost 2-3 against defending champions China. India were not clinical enough in their final group stage tie and ended up losing matches in deciding games.

Ryan Rickelton puts SRH bowlers to sword, hits maiden IPL hundredMI vs SRH: Ryan Rickelton provided Mumbai Indians with the perfect foundation to make a comeback in the Indian Premier League. Playing against SRH, Rickelton hit a 44-ball hundred at the Wankhede Stadium.

Kanteerava Stadium turns white after intense hailstorm sweeps BengaluruA sudden hailstorm in Bengaluru turned Sree Kanteerava Stadium white within minutes. Viral videos had fans joking it looked like snowfall in summer.

Rohit Sharma misses 4th straight IPL 2026 match: What's gone wrong hitman?IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma missed his fourth straight IPL 2026 match as Mumbai Indians faced Sunrisers Hyderabad. Hardik Pandya said the opener needs a couple more games, extending uncertainty around MI's batting.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
